review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Pure dictatorship at Haridwar and Halol Plant. Culture driven from top management. Zero Work Life Balance. 

2. You have to be the 'yes man' in order to survive 

3. All the appraisal ratings are fixed. Even HR is least supportive over here. Your ratings are independent of the work you perform and are totally dependent on how good you are in the books of senior management.

4. Consider your career as finished once you get a bad rating. Your growth will be stalled for next 5 years minimum. So join these plants at your own risk.

5. HR during recruitment cheat with the candidates by offering them lowest possible compensations and lowest possible designation by saying that such designation can't be offered at your experience while a less experienced person is currently working at higher pay scale and better designation in the same Department.

6. Senior management only gives good grades and promotion to middle management while the lower managers and engineers down the line receive the leftovers.

7. All the internal job postings and rotations depend only on the wish of seniormost manager. If he denies it then you either rot in your position or you leave the organisation. There's no third option.

8. Bosses superficially show that they empathize with you but you are nothing but cash cows for them.
